subject: Statistics
title: CIs vs. P-Values
description: A quick, practical guide to CIs vs. P-Values.

hook.text: A p-value says 'significant or not.' A confidence interval says 'how much, and how uncertain.'

buildup.text: If a 95% CI for the difference doesn't include zero, it's significant at the 0.05 level. They're linked.

discovery.text: But the CI also shows the effect size and precision. It's a richer, more informative summary than p alone.

twist.text: You can have a significant p-value with a CI so wide it's practically useless. The p-value hides this.

climax.text: Many statisticians argue we should report CIs instead of p-values. They show everything p-values do and more.

punchline.text: P-values give a verdict. CIs tell the whole story.

quiz.question: What advantage do confidence intervals have over p-values?
options: They show both the effect size and precision, not just whether a result is significant
